name: Sealed Research Tablet
description: A smooth stone tablet the size of your palm. It's warm to the touch and covered in precise, tiny notation that shifts when you're not looking directly at it. A seal mark on the reverse — not a temple seal. Something personal.

effects.description: the tablet grows warmer and the notation glows faintly

lore: Research notes from an Aelindran outpost that was sealed long before the Sundering. The notation system is non-standard — personal, not academic. Whoever wrote this didn't want it found in the official archives.

name: Hollow-Bone Fragment
description: A shard of something that might be bone. It's cold — not room-temperature cold, actively cold, as if it's drawing heat from the air around it. It makes a sound like a tuning fork when struck, but the pitch is wrong. Not a frequency you've heard before.

effects.description: nearby plants wilt slowly; animals avoid the carrier

lore: Salvaged from a Hollow creature after combat. Scholars in the Accord would pay well for a sample — intact Hollow material is rare this far south.

name: Torn Journal Page
description: A torn page of densely written notation on thin, age-darkened vellum. The handwriting is precise but hurried. Some phrases are legible: 'resonance thinning,' 'controlled aperture,' 'acceptable threshold.' The notation system isn't standard academic — it's personal, idiosyncratic.
lore: A fragment from a research journal found in the Greyvale Ruins. The notation matches no known academic standard — whoever wrote this used a personal system, suggesting secrecy or extreme specialization.

name: Guild Investigation Contract
description: A formal contract bearing the Accord Guild's seal. It authorizes the bearer to investigate reports of Hollow activity in the Greyvale region. Torin's signature is at the bottom — heavy-handed, impatient.
lore: An official guild contract. Having one opens doors that a stranger's word alone wouldn't.

name: Healing Potion
description: A small clay vial sealed with wax. The liquid inside is faintly warm and smells of copper and something sweet you can't name.
lore: Standard healing draught, brewed by temple herbalists. The recipe hasn't changed in centuries.

name: Hollow Ward Charm
description: A small carved bone pendant on a leather cord. It's warm to the touch, always, regardless of temperature. When Hollow creatures are near, it vibrates faintly against your chest.

effects.description: Vibrates faintly when Hollow creatures are within earshot

lore: Carved from the rib bone of a creature that survived exposure to the Hollow. Elder Yanna's grandmother made these — most people thought she was superstitious. Most people were wrong.

name: Millhaven Provisions
description: A cloth bundle of dried meat, hard bread, and a small flask of clear spirits. Simple food, given with gratitude.

effects.description: Provides comfort during a short rest

lore: Provisions from Millhaven's stores. Not fancy, but given freely — which in a town this scared means something.

name: Humming Stone
description: A smooth, palm-sized stone that is always warm. Hold it near a flame and it hums — a low, steady note that you feel in your teeth more than hear. The surface has a faint iridescence, like oil on water, and when you're not looking directly at it, shapes seem to move beneath the surface.

effects.description: the stone grows warmer and the humming intensifies
prolonged contact causes vivid dreams about places that don't exist

lore: A fragment of an Attenuation Sphere — one of Veythar's artifacts for weakening the Veil. Aldric found it in the Greyvale Ruins and it partially opened his mind to the Veil. The stone is still active, which is why the Hollow corruption in the Greyvale is spreading.

name: Shortsword
description: A simple blade, well-balanced and sharp enough. Nothing fancy — the kind of sword that does its job without complaining about it.
lore: Standard-issue for guild recruits and city guards.

name: Guild Longsword
description: A well-made longsword bearing a small guild mark near the crossguard. Heavier than it looks, balanced for someone who means business.
lore: Grimjaw's standard work. Functional, reliable, and as honest as the dwarf who forged it.

name: Balanced Dagger
description: Light and well-weighted, suitable for close quarters or throwing. The edge catches lamplight.
lore: A simple dagger. Every adventurer carries one.

name: Leather Armor
description: Cured leather, stiffened and shaped to cover the torso. Smells of tanning oil and honest work. Won't stop a heavy blow, but it's quiet and doesn't slow you down.
lore: Standard light armor. Preferred by scouts, rogues, and anyone who values mobility.

name: Chain Shirt
description: Interlocking metal rings woven into a shirt. Heavier than leather, quieter than plate. Grimjaw's work — each link is even and tight.
lore: Grimjaw's chain work. He considers it beneath him but it pays the bills.

name: Iron Shield
description: A round shield with an iron boss and wooden body. Dented from the proving rack. Solid.
lore: A reliable shield. Not decorative, not special. Just good work.

name: Trail Rations
description: Dried meat, hard biscuit, a handful of nuts. Tastes like nothing but keeps you walking.
lore: Standard travel food. Sold by every merchant on the coast.

name: Hemp Rope
description: Fifty feet of coiled hemp rope. Smells of tar and the harbor.
lore: Always carry rope. Everyone who forgot wishes they hadn't.

name: Bundle of Torches
description: Three torches wrapped in oilcloth. Each burns for about an hour.

effects.description: Provides bright light in a 20-foot radius

lore: Fire keeps the dark honest.

name: Waterskin
description: A leather waterskin, stoppered and full. The water tastes faintly of leather and salt.
lore: Fresh water. Underrated until you don't have it.

name: Basic Antidote
description: A small vial of murky green liquid. Tastes like chewed grass and regret. Works fast.
lore: Temple-brewed. Herbalists stock them, and experienced travelers never travel without one.

name: Holy Water
description: A small crystal vial of water blessed by temple clergy. It catches light in a way plain water doesn't and is faintly warm to the touch.

effects.description: Burns Hollow creatures on contact — the water sizzles and the creature recoils

lore: Water blessed through a ritual older than the temples themselves. Effective against Hollow creatures — one of the few things that is.

name: Restoration Salve
description: A small tin of pale ointment that smells of clean linen and something floral. Applied to skin, it absorbs quickly and leaves warmth behind.

effects.description: Heals minor wounds and removes one status effect

lore: Temple-made restorative. Useful for burns, minor corruption exposure, and the general wear of adventuring.
